summary: High‑energy set with Shakedown Street, Estimated Prophet & soaring Franklin’s Tower
blurb: The concert opens strong and rolls into a fiery Shakedown Street, then expands with an expansive Estimated Prophet that builds to a climactic Franklin’s Tower. Jerry’s voice shows early strain from recent laryngitis but his guitar stays on point, while Brent Mydland delivers a solid debut on keys. Highlights also include Passenger, Black Peter and the seamless Miracle‑Bertha‑Good Lovin’ medley.
review: May 11, 1979 at the Billerica Forum captured one of those high‑octane spring nights that still feels fresh in the ears. The first set kicks off with a tight Mississippi Half‑Step before diving into a soaring Franklin’s Tower and a seamless Mama Tried → Mexicali Blues segue that keeps the momentum rolling. By the second set the band is fully locked in: Shakedown Street erupts with Jerry’s searing lead, Passenger stretches out with a lyrical solo, and the Estimated Prophet/Eyes of the World sequence launches into an exploratory jam that culminates in a transcendent Franklin’s Tower. Despite a hoarse start to his vocals – a lingering effect of recent laryngitis – Garcia’s guitar work remains fiery, especially on Shakedown and Passenger, while Brent Mydland’s debut on keys blends beautifully with the band’s texture. Phil’s bass provides a solid foundation even as it dips in the mix later, and the dual‑drum engine of Mickey and Bill drives the grooves forward. The night is capped by a soulful Black Peter and an uplifting Miracle → Bertha → Good Lovin’ medley that leaves the crowd buzzing. Among the available tapes, the Nakamichi 700 audience recording (gd1979-05-11.nak700.rolfe.miller.99029.sbeok.flac16) offers crystal‑clear ambience and captures the show’s energy better than any other source, making it the definitive listen for this performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive; reviewers repeatedly praise the high‑energy set, standout jams on Shakedown Street, Estimated Prophet and Franklin’s Tower, as well as Brent's solid debut. Only a single note of a weak Eyes Of The World performance appears, which does not outweigh the overall enthusiasm.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience capture with warm ambience, crisp detail and the most complete representation of the show’s energy.

key_highlights: High‑energy second set featuring Estimated Prophet into a climactic Franklin’s Tower
Standout jams on Shakedown Street, Passenger and Black Peter
Brent Mydland’s solid debut on keys despite Jerry’s vocal strain
Unusually detailed audience recording quality that rivals soundboard tapes

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals were strained early from recent laryngitis, but his guitar remained strong and he delivered powerful high notes later; solos on Shakedown Street and Passenger were praised despite a tentative first solo.
band_performance.Phil: Bass lines were audible early in the set before fading slightly; noted for using a quirky “submarine oscillator” rig.
band_performance.Brent: Debut welcomed – sounds solid and blends well with the band even as Jerry dealt with vocal strain.
